---
title: "peanut_butter"
author: "Ruwen Zhou"
date: "4/3/2025"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(readr)
library(plyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(phyloseq)
library(vegan)
library(DESeq2)
library(pheatmap)
library(RColorBrewer)
```

```{r}
food_data800 <- read_excel("data/all_food_863.xlsx")
scfa800 <- read_excel("data/scfa_species800.xlsx")
species_data_800 = read_excel("data/species_866.xlsx")
food_data800
scfa800
species_data_800
```
```{r}
merge_butyrate_peanut = merge(food_data800[,c("participant_id", "peanut_butter")], species_data_800, by.x = "participant_id", by.y = "FREG1_Barcode", all = FALSE)
merge_butyrate_peanut
```
```{r}
# Calculate correlations between peanut_butter and all microbiome species

# First, let's check the structure of the dataframe
#str(merge_butyrate_peanut)

# Get the column names to identify which are the species columns
species_columns <- colnames(merge_butyrate_peanut)[-(1:2)]  # Excluding participant_id and peanut_butter

# Create an empty dataframe to store results
correlation_results <- data.frame(
  species = character(),
  correlation = numeric(),
  p_value = numeric(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Calculate correlation for each species with peanut_butter
for (species in species_columns) {
  # Calculate Pearson correlation and p-value
  cor_test <- cor.test(merge_butyrate_peanut$peanut_butter, 
                      merge_butyrate_peanut[[species]], 
                      method = "spearman")
  
  # Add results to the dataframe
  correlation_results <- rbind(correlation_results, 
                              data.frame(species = species,
                                        correlation = cor_test$estimate,
                                        p_value = cor_test$p.value))
}

# Sort by absolute correlation (strongest correlations first)
correlation_results <- correlation_results[order(-abs(correlation_results$correlation)), ]

# Adjust p-values for multiple testing using Benjamini-Hochberg method
correlation_results$adjusted_p_value <- p.adjust(correlation_results$p_value, method = "BH")

# Filter for significant correlations (adjusted p < 0.05)
significant_correlations <- correlation_results[correlation_results$adjusted_p_value < 0.05, ]

# Display the top correlations
head(correlation_results, 20)

# Display significant correlations
print("Significant correlations after multiple testing correction:")
significant_correlations
correlation_results
```
```{r}
# Define genera known to contain butyrate producers
butyrate_genera <- c(
  "Faecalibacterium",
  "Eubacterium",
  "Roseburia",
  "Anaerostipes",
  "Coprococcus",
  "Butyricicoccus",
  "Clostridium",
  "Ruminococcus",
  "Anaerobutyricum"
)

# Get all column names from the dataframe (excluding participant_id and peanut_butter)
all_species_cols <- colnames(merge_butyrate_peanut)[-(1:2)]

# Function to check if a column name contains any of the butyrate genera
contains_butyrate_genus <- function(column_name) {
  for(genus in butyrate_genera) {
    if(grepl(genus, column_name, ignore.case = TRUE)) {
      return(TRUE)
    }
  }
  return(FALSE)
}

# Filter for columns that contain butyrate-producing genera
butyrate_species_cols <- all_species_cols[sapply(all_species_cols, contains_butyrate_genus)]

# Print the identified butyrate-producing species
cat("Identified butyrate-producing species in the dataset:\n")
print(butyrate_species_cols)
cat("\nTotal number of butyrate-producing species found:", length(butyrate_species_cols), "\n\n")

# Now calculate correlations only for these butyrate-producing species
butyrate_correlations <- data.frame(
  species = character(),
  correlation = numeric(),
  p_value = numeric(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Calculate correlation for each butyrate-producing species with peanut_butter
for (species in butyrate_species_cols) {
  # Calculate Pearson correlation and p-value
  cor_test <- cor.test(merge_butyrate_peanut$peanut_butter, 
                      merge_butyrate_peanut[[species]], 
                      method = "pearson")
  
  # Add results to the dataframe
  butyrate_correlations <- rbind(butyrate_correlations, 
                                data.frame(species = species,
                                          correlation = cor_test$estimate,
                                          p_value = cor_test$p.value))
}

# Sort by absolute correlation (strongest correlations first)
butyrate_correlations <- butyrate_correlations[order(-abs(butyrate_correlations$correlation)), ]

# Adjust p-values for multiple testing using Benjamini-Hochberg method
butyrate_correlations$adjusted_p_value <- p.adjust(butyrate_correlations$p_value, method = "BH")

# Display all butyrate correlations
print("Correlations between peanut butter and butyrate-producing species:")
print(butyrate_correlations)
```
```{r}
library(writexl)
file_path <- "data/butyrate_peanut_correlations.xlsx"

write_xlsx(butyrate_correlations, file_path)
```

```{r}
butyrate_correlations[butyrate_correlations$correlation > 0, ]
```



```{r}
# Filter for significant correlations (adjusted p < 0.05)
significant_butyrate_correlations <- butyrate_correlations[butyrate_correlations$p_value < 0.05, ]

# Display significant correlations
if(nrow(significant_butyrate_correlations) > 0) {
  cat("\nSignificant correlations after multiple testing correction:\n")
  print(significant_butyrate_correlations)
} else {
  cat("\nNo significant correlations found after multiple testing correction.\n")
}


```
